## Task: Fix [high] UI/UX: ARIA menu pattern incomplete: no role=menu/menuitem, no aria-controls, no focus management, no arrow
## Step: review-tests

You are a fix agent. Resolve the following final-review finding.

Review dimension: UI/UX
Severity: high
File: src/app/dom-builders.ts:131-245
Title: ARIA menu pattern incomplete: no role=menu/menuitem, no aria-controls, no focus management, no arrow-key nav

Finding:
The trigger uses aria-haspopup='true' and toggles aria-expanded, but: (1) it has no aria-controls pointing to the menu id, so assistive tech cannot associate the button with its popup; (2) the .row-menu container has no role='menu' and the items have no role='menuitem' (they are a plain div containing an <a> and <button>s), so screen readers do not announce a menu or menu-item semantics; (3) focus is not moved into the menu when it opens (neither the button click nor the contextmenu handler calls .focus()), so keyboard/screen-reader users are left on the trigger; (4) there is no Arrow Up/Down/Enter/Escape keyboard navigation between items (only default Tab order, which is non-standard for a menu). This degrades the experience for assistive-tech users below the expected ARIA Authoring Practices Guide menu-button/menu pattern.

Fix instructions (follow exactly; make targeted, minimal changes):
In src/app/dom-builders.ts makeRowMenu, complete the ARIA menu pattern. (a) Give the menu a stable id (e.g. `menu.id = 'row-menu-' + <unique per row>`) and set `btn.setAttribute('aria-controls', menu.id)`. (b) Set `menu.setAttribute('role', 'menu')`. (c) Set `role='menuitem'` on each appended action element (the Download <a> and each makeActionButton result). (d) On open (inside openMenu, after revealing the menu), move focus to the first menuitem: `const first = menu.querySelector('[role=menuitem]'); first?.focus();`. (e) Add a keydown handler on the menu that implements Arrow Down/Up to move focus among menuitems (wrap around), Enter/Space to activate the focused item (default behavior already does this for button/a, so mainly preventDefault for arrows and move focus), and Escape to close and return focus to the trigger button (call closeMenu() then btn.focus()). Keep the existing close-on-document-click and the existing item click handlers intact; this is purely additive aria attributes + focus/arrow-key handling. Update src/app/render-browse.test.ts only if any existing assertion checks attribute sets that would conflict (none currently do).

## Review Feedback History (please address all items)
Attempt 1: The test file doesn't exist or hasn't been updated to cover the ARIA improvements. Without test coverage verification, the implementation may have regressions or incomplete testing.

Finding:
The trigger uses aria-haspopup='true' and toggles aria-expanded, but: (1) it has no aria-controls pointing to the menu id, so assistive tech cannot associate the button with its popup; (2) the .row-menu container has no role='menu' and the items have no role='menuitem' (they are a plain div containing an <a> and <button>s), so screen readers do not announce a menu or menu-item semantics; (3) focus is not moved into the menu when it opens (neither the button click nor the contextmenu handler calls .focus()), so keyboard/screen-reader users are left on the trigger; (4) there is no Arrow Up/Down/Enter/Escape keyboard navigation between items (only default Tab order, which is non-standard for a menu). This degrades the experience for assistive-tech users below the expected ARIA Authoring Practices Guide menu-button/menu pattern.

Fix instructions (follow exactly; make targeted, minimal changes):

(c) Set `role='menuitem'` on each appended action element (the Download <a> and each makeActionButton result). (d) On open (inside openMenu, after revealing the menu), move focus to the first menuitem: `const first = menu.querySelector('[role=menuitem]'); first?.focus();`. (e) Add a keydown handler on the menu that implements Arrow Down/Up to move focus among menuitems (wrap around), Enter/Space to activate the focused item (default behavior already does this for button/a, so mainly preventDefault for arrows and move focus), and Escape to close and return focus to the trigger button (call closeMenu() then btn.focus()).
